objective: Implement the bounded inner agent loop (run_explore) in core::explore with mode-steering data, harness-enforced tool gating, and a three-phase Balanced state machine — all backed by unit tests using a scripted fake ChatClient.
key_changes: New core/src/explore/agent.rs: run_explore() loop, ExploreAnswer, ExploreError, phase machine for Balanced mode
New core/src/explore/toolset.rs: Tool schema registry for 7 structural ops, toolset builders per phase, dispatch (ops + shell), corrective gating
New core/src/explore/steering.rs: per-mode system prompt text as static data (Standard/Aggressive/Balanced phase-1/Balanced phase-2)
Edit core/src/explore/mod.rs: declare 3 new modules, re-export run_explore, ExploreAnswer, ExploreError
Tool gating at two levels: absent from tools[] array sent to model, plus corrective tool-result message on hallucinated calls
Balanced mode harness: phase 1 = RECON_OPS + submit_plan; after N recon turns = submit_plan only; phase 2 = full toolset + plan hint injected into system message
Shell dispatch uses std::process::Command with args vector — no shell interpolation; binary validated against cfg.allowed_tools before execution
Turn bound (MAX_TURNS=25) and byte bound (MAX_TOOL_RESULT_BYTES=128 KiB) produce Ok(ExploreAnswer{truncated:true}), not an error
ClientError::Connection maps to ExploreError::ProviderDown as required by AC-6 / D3
10 unit tests with scripted fake ChatClient: hallucinated tool, per-mode toolset assertions, balanced phase transitions, allowlist refusal, turn/byte bound termination, provider-down mapping
No new Cargo.toml dependencies; no clap; no fastcontext; warning-clean and clippy-clean
